headline: Chip rally valuation risk at highest since 2015
detail: The article flags that the semiconductor sector's concentration and valuation extreme is at a level not seen since 2015 - a historically reliable warning sign for a correction or sharp rotation. For your book this is direct: SMH, SOXL, and most of your holdings are in the exact sector being flagged. On margin, a sector-wide pullback hits you harder than almost any other trader type.
rec: Consider trimming SOXL first - it's 3x levered, decays over time, and is your highest-risk chip exposure. If you want to reduce sector risk more broadly, SMH is the cleaner way to lighten chips without leverage. No scanner long setup supports adding chip exposure here.

headline: Cleveland Fed: AI could fuel inflation, rate hikes may return
detail: A Fed president publicly flagging that AI-driven demand could reignite inflation and force rate hikes is a meaningful headwind for high-multiple tech and chip names. Rate hike expectations compress valuations fastest in exactly the growth names you hold - CRWD, QQQ, SMH, SOXL. This also raises the cost of your margin directly if rates move higher.
rec: No immediate trade, but this is a reason not to add leverage or new positions today. If rate-hike rhetoric builds over the next few sessions, CRWD (your largest single-name at +6%) would be the first trim candidate - it's already in daily premium per the scanner and not offering a discount entry.

headline: Nvidia mostly sat out chips' best quarter - what needs to change?
detail: If the dominant name in the chip rally underperformed during its best quarter, that raises a real question about whether the next leg higher has a credible leader. NVDA not participating is a yellow flag for SMH and SOXL, both of which carry heavy NVDA weighting. It doesn't break the trend but it's a reason not to chase.
rec: No action today, but watch SMH and SOXL for any follow-through weakness. The scanner shows no fresh discount entry in chip ETFs right now, so there's no setup to justify adding.

headline: AWS puts $1 billion into new AI unit
detail: Amazon embedding AI engineers directly with enterprise customers is a signal that cloud-AI spending is accelerating and becoming more sticky. This is broadly supportive of the AI infrastructure theme that underpins CRWD and the semiconductor names you hold. It's a tailwind, not a catalyst for a same-day trade.
rec: No trade needed - your existing holdings already capture this theme. CRWD has a scanner setup (entry 758.92-760.27, stop 755.60) but it's in daily premium meaning you'd be chasing; only relevant if you want to add to a winner with a very tight stop and small size.
